label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out the best title for this passage. Let me read through the passage again to understand the main idea. The passage starts by saying that our predispositions towards determination, sociability, self-control, and sense of purpose are largely genetic. It mentions that DNA plays a bigger role than upbringing or the people we keep. The study used twins to compare identical (same DNA) and non-identical twins to determine the influence of genetics versus environment. The results showed that genes have a bigger impact, especially for self-control. The professor, Timothy Bates, talks about how genetics is a more powerful influence on psychological well-being than previously thought. But he also advises that even if you don't have the best genes, having a sense of purpose is key and focusing on making a difference is important. Now, looking at the options: (A) How do people work hard? – This seems too general. The passage is about why some people succeed, not specifically about working hard. (B) Why can some people succeed? – This is a possibility. The passage does discuss factors (genes) that contribute to success. (C) DNA determines success or failure – This is also a contender. The passage emphasizes the role of DNA in personality traits that lead to success or failure. (D) Several factors determine
